title: Crispy MiddleEast Almond Flatbread
description: A quick, crunchy flatbread with toasted almond crunch, finished with a splash of sweet sherry for depth—perfect for a lazy Sunday when you crave something salty and satisfying.

ingredients.name: fresh parsley, chopped
ingredients.notes: optional garnish, low FODMAP in this amount

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Whisk flour, ground almonds, baking powder, and salt in a medium bowl until combined.
In a separate bowl, combine lukewarm water, olive oil, sweet sherry, oregano, and cumin.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Transfer dough to the parchment-lined sheet and use a rolling pin to roll it out to about 1/4 inch thickness.
Cut the dough into squares or triangles using a sharp knife or pizza cutter.
Brush the tops lightly with a little extra olive oil to promote crisping.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crisp.
Remove from oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley if desired. Serve warm.
Serve immediately or store in an airtight container for up to 2 days.
